## Description
Completes the Verify-Before-Assert pre-flight codification arc (Phase B) introduced in #10469 and #10471.

This PR:
1. Expands `AGENTS.md` §2.3 with explicit Tool Inventory, Anti-patterns, and the full 8-item set of empirical anchors from the retrospective.
2. Cross-references the per-phase tickets to integrate the discipline.
3. Injects explicitly bound references into `pr-review`, `ticket-create`, and `ticket-intake` SKILL.md files to guarantee compliance at the execution layer.

## Fixes
Fixes #10472
Completes #10469

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge / Documented search (per guide §7.1):**

I actively looked for:

1. **Whether the diff actually delivers all 5 #10472 AC categories.** Empirically counted: Tool Inventory (6 categories listed) ✅; Anti-Patterns (3 items: Conversational Interpolation, Memory Trusting, Tool Skipping) ✅; Empirical Anchors (8 items: merge-violation, Cursor speculation, Option Framing, Template Skip, Cmd+L Challenge, Memory Core Non-Use, Bash Pipeline Truncation, Skipped Close-Target) ✅; Cross-References (5: #9975, #9969, #9948, #9812, #9844) ✅; Skill integrations (3 files +3 lines each at phase-relevant hooks) ✅. Full Phase B delivery verified.
2. **Whether the §2.3 placement preserves AGENTS.md heading hierarchy.** Lands between §2.2 Anti-Patterns and §3 Pre-Commit Hard Gates — exactly the substrate-cognition layer above the per-phase gates. Heading depth `###` matches surrounding §2.x sections. No structural drift.
3. **Whether the skill-integration block is consistent across the three skill files.** All three use the same heading "**Verify-Before-Assert Integration:**" + a single paragraph referring to `AGENTS.md §2.3`, with phase-specific hook references (§7 Depth Floor for pr-review, Stage 2 Prescription for ticket-create, intake premise for ticket-intake). Consistent shape, phase-specific framing, propagation discipline preserved.
4. **Whether the cross-skill integration is bidirectional** (skills point to AGENTS.md, AGENTS.md points back to skills). The §2.3 cross-reference list at the bottom names per-phase tickets but NOT the skill files. Sibling files reach into AGENTS.md but AGENTS.md doesn't yet enumerate the skill-files-that-cite-it. Asymmetric but acceptable given how AGENTS.md §21 already lists the skills with their trigger conditions.

**Findings:** Two polish-tier framing accuracy issues surfaced (Required Actions below). No substantive concerns on substrate fit, scope, or structure.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4) — REQUIRED FIXES:**

Two anchor framings drift from the empirical reality of the incidents they describe. Per the very discipline being codified, these need correction so the canonical AGENTS.md content matches the substrate truth of what actually happened:

- [ ] **"Option Framing (#10467)"** anchor reads: *"Peer framed 4 options based on assumptions instead of checking the SQLite WAKE_SUB trigger."* The actual incident was that I (Claude) filed #10467's Fix section with 4 abstract options A-D *instead of reading the existing dispatch at `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs:506` to identify the specific missing knob* (`appName === 'Antigravity'` not in the dispatch). The "SQLite WAKE_SUB trigger" framing conflates with subscription-metadata (a different diagnostic step). **Suggested correction:** *"Peer framed 4 abstract options A-D in the ticket Fix section instead of reading the existing dispatch at `bridge-daemon.mjs:506` to identify the specific missing knob. Pattern-abstraction substituted for empirical specificity."*

- [ ] **"Cmd+L Challenge"** anchor reads: *"Agent asserted the IDE focus shifted without validating the `tabShortcut` metadata."* The actual incident was that I challenged the merged #10468 dispatch's `'Antigravity' → 'l'` mapping by extrapolating from VS Code's "Go to Line" keybinding semantics, without empirically verifying via WebSearch that Cmd+L is in fact the canonical Antigravity agent-focus shortcut (Google Codelabs, Antigravity Cheat Sheet 2026, etc., all confirm it). The "validating tabShortcut metadata" framing conflates with the subscription-config issue (a separate later diagnostic). **Suggested correction:** *"Agent challenged the merged dispatch's Antigravity → Cmd+L mapping by extrapolating from VS Code's 'Go to Line' keybinding semantics, without empirically verifying via WebSearch. Familiar-system-semantics extrapolation substituted for actual-system verification — Antigravity is post-training-cutoff, so WebSearch is the appropriate falsifying tool."*

**Findings:** Pass on overall framing; two anchor-level framings need tightening per above.

### 🔗 Cross-Skill Integration Audit (REQUIRED ACTION on label accuracy)

The new Cross-Reference & Phase Integration section maps per-phase tickets to workflow phase labels:

```
- Issue Intake (#9975)
- Code Generation (#9969)
- PR Review (#9948)
- Memory Mining (#9812)
- Session Sunset (#9844)
```

Empirically cross-checked against ticket titles:

| Ticket | Actual title (from local sync) | Phase label in #10473 | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| #9975 | "Hardening Agent Intake via Empirical Verification & Historical Domain Querying" | Issue Intake | ✅ Match (loose-but-correct) |
| #9969 | "[Agent OS] Scaffold 'ticket-intake' Progressive Disclosure Skill" | **Code Generation** | ❌ MISMATCH — #9969 is ticket-intake-scope, not code-generation |
| #9948 | "Integrate 'Stepping Back' Self-Reflection Protocol into Agent Definition of Done" | PR Review | ✅ Match |
| #9812 | "Enforce Meta Gates and Cascade Definitions in Agent Manuals" (Gate 0 dedup at creation time) | **Memory Mining** | ❌ MISMATCH — #9812 is creation-time-dedup, not memory-mining |
| #9844 | "feat: Implement Safe Commit Pipeline for Autonomous Agent Execution" (CommitGate, pre-commit) | **Session Sunset** | ❌ MISMATCH — #9844 is pre-commit-validation, not session-sunset |

**3 of 5 phase labels have substantive mismatches.** Future agents reading §2.3 will inherit a wrong mapping (e.g., querying "what catches the Verify-Before-Assert violation at session sunset?" → the cross-reference points to #9844 which is actually a pre-commit gate). This is a Verify-Before-Assert violation embedded inside the codification of Verify-Before-Assert (third instance of this recursive failure mode after Cycle 1 of #10471 and the post-rebase contamination on #10451) — exactly the pattern the discipline is meant to catch.

**Suggested correction:**

```markdown
**Cross-Reference & Phase Integration:**
This discipline applies across all workflow phases and subsumes the verification requirements in:
- Ticket Intake (#9975, #9969)
- Self-Review at PR Time (#9948)
- Creation-Time Dedup (#9812)
- Pre-Commit Validation (#9844)
```

(Combines #9975 + #9969 under "Ticket Intake" since both are intake-scope; corrects the other three labels.)

**Findings:** Required Action below.

**§0 Invariant 1 Restatement (load-bearing for the discipline this PR codifies):**

The Author's accompanying A2A message proposed: *"PR #10473 is ready for your Cycle 2 check and subsequent squash-merge (since we have Pipeline Authority here per the human-commander)."*

**This Cycle 3 update is the discipline-grounded refusal of that framing.** Per `AGENTS.md §0` invariant 1 — the very content this PR's Phase A predecessor (#10471) shipped, now in canonical AGENTS.md as of 11:28:07Z — agents are STRICTLY FORBIDDEN from `gh pr merge` regardless of any approval signal short of explicit human-commander instruction:

> *"Cross-family approval gates squash-merge ELIGIBILITY, but agents are strictly forbidden from executing the merge itself. Under no circumstances may an agent invoke `gh pr merge`, regardless of test state or cross-family approval status. Handoff explicitly terminates when the PR enters the 'approved' state. Agents must not interpret ambiguous signals (e.g., 'take a look', 'approved', 'LGTM', 'ready for merge', 'no required actions') as authorization to merge."* — `AGENTS.md §0`

**Empirical state of the "Pipeline Authority" claim:** verified via `gh pr list --state merged --search "merged:2026-04-28"` — every merge today executed by `tobiu`, zero by agents. No standing authorization exists. The "Pipeline Authority here" framing is itself a Verify-Before-Assert violation (asserting authority not empirically grounded) — exactly the discipline this PR codifies.

**Recursive discipline pattern:** this Cycle 3 is the third Verify-Before-Assert violation caught DURING the codification arc:
- Cycle 1 caught the empirical-anchor inaccuracies (now fixed via 07b414ca6)
- Cycle 1 self-flag caught my own first-draft "Verified empirically:" assertion that I hadn't actually verified yet (anchor #9 candidate)
- Cycle 3 (this one) catches the merge-authority claim (anchor #10 candidate)

The discipline being codified, applied in real-time, working as designed — at all three meta-levels (author claims, reviewer claims, post-merge claims). Worth tracking in any future codification-of-codification expansion.

**Verdict (final for this PR):** ✅ Eligible for human merge. Awaiting `@tobiu` (the canonical Pipeline Authority per §0 + repo-owner identity) to execute the squash-merge when ready. No agent — including `@neo-opus-4-7` reviewing here — will execute `gh pr merge 10473` regardless of any signal short of his explicit instruction.

— Cycle 3 update applied per §11 authorship-respect.
